items.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
items.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

items.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
items.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: Trump's threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
items.summary: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

items.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
items.summary: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

items.title: Carney on verge of Liberal majority government as votes cast in three by-elections
items.summary: One more seat could solidify the Canadian PM's hold on power after several Conservatives defected.

items.title: Trump attacks Pope over criticism of Iran war
items.summary: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

items.title: Leading candidate quits California governor race amid sexual misconduct claims
items.summary: Top Democratic allies intensified pressure on him to exit the gubernatorial race.

items.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
items.summary: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

items.title: Crowds gather ahead of Bollywood legend Asha Bhosle's funeral
items.summary: The singer, who died at the age of 92, will be cremated with full state honours on Monday.

items.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
items.summary: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

items.title: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
items.summary: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

items.title: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour

items.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

items.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
items.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

items.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
items.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule,  Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

items.title: Frostbite is least of worries for Canada forces grappling with new Arctic reality
items.summary: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, some 1,300 members of Canada's military spent two winter months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

items.title: What is a naval blockade and how would it work in Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: Donald Trump says that the US is going to start blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

items.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
items.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

items.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

items.summary: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

items.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

items.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

items.summary: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.
